resources.string: Switch between AR Projection, Overlay, Mockup, and Trace modes.
Project your artwork onto a real wall using augmented reality. Scan the wall first to place the anchor, then add your design layers.
Layer your design over the live camera feed without AR anchoring. Great for quick framing and placement checks.
Preview your design composited onto a photo of a wall. No camera or scanning required.
Use your design as a light-table overlay to trace reference lines onto a physical surface.
Switch between Canvas (optimized for smaller desk-scale art) and Mural (optimized for large-scale walls and Voxel-based spatial tracking).
Generate printable multi-layer stencils from your artwork. Each layer prints as outline-only tiles sized for US Letter paper.
Create and manage AR anchor targets. Photograph painted reference marks on the wall to lock the projection in place.
Map the wall surface to improve AR tracking accuracy.
Photograph a reference mark on the wall to create a new AR anchor. The app will track this mark to keep your projection aligned.
Host a co-op session to share your AR coordinate system with nearby peers.
Join a nearby co-op session to align your AR view with the host.
Connect to smart glasses for a hands-free AR experience and perspective-aligned camera input.
Fine-tune the position of an existing AR anchor target.
Mark completed areas of your mural to track painting progress.
Add and manage design layers. Each layer can be an imported image or a freehand drawing.
Add an image from your gallery as a new layer.
Replace the background wall photo used in Mockup mode.
Remove the background wall photo used in Mockup mode.
Automatically remove the background from this layer, leaving only the subject on a transparent background.
Create a negative of the current layer. Blacks become whites, and colors are flipped to their opposites — useful for stencil and overlay analysis.
Convert this layer to black line art on a transparent background — ideal for creating stencil templates.
Fine-tune brightness, contrast, saturation, and color balance for this layer.
Tint the layer colors using red, green, and blue channel sliders.
Cycle through blend modes (Normal, Multiply, Screen, Overlay…) to control how this layer composites with layers below.
Configure handedness, AR scan mode, units, and other app preferences.
Manage your project: create, save, load, and export.
Start fresh empty project. Any unsaved changes will be lost.
Save the current project to your device.
Open the project library to load a previously saved project.
Export a flat merged image of all visible layers to your photo gallery.
Tap any button while Help is active to see what it does.
Toggle the device flashlight to illuminate your wall while working in low light.
Lock the touchscreen to prevent accidental changes while you spray. Triple-tap anywhere to unlock.
Paint freehand strokes on this layer. Drag up/down on the Size button to change brush diameter; drag left/right to adjust feathering (edge softness).
Erase parts of this layer with a round brush. Drag up/down on the Size button to change diameter; drag left/right to adjust feathering.
Smear and soften areas of this layer. Drag across a region to blur it.
Push and warp pixels in any direction. Drag across an area to distort it — useful for adjusting proportions.
Lighten specific areas by painting over them.
Darken specific areas by painting over them.
Drag up/down to change brush diameter. Drag left/right to adjust feathering — a wider feather gives softer, more spray-can-like edges.
Shows the active brush color. Drag up/down to adjust lightness; drag left/right to adjust saturation. Tap to open the full color wheel.
Add a new blank transparent layer for freehand drawing.
Save the current AR camera pose as a keyframe. Used to correct drift and re-align the projection after moving away and returning.
This layer. Tap to activate it. Open the horizontal rail to access tools. Long-press and drag to reorder.
Choose how many stencil layers to generate: 1 (silhouette only), 2 (silhouette + highlight), or 3 (silhouette + midtone + highlight).
Re-run the stencil generation pipeline on the current source layer.
Configure the real-world print size and generate a tiled PDF for US Letter paper.
Drag up/down to set the stencil\'s real-world width or height in centimetres. Page count updates live.
Tap to switch between locking the width or the height of the output.
Generate a tiled PDF of all stencil layers ready to print and cut. Each tile includes a 3mm overlap for assembly alignment.
Save each stencil layer as a separate PNG image to your gallery.
Return to the main editor. Your stencil layers are preserved in the session.
Double tap to switch rotation axis.
Press again to add a layer.
How would you like to set the background wall?
This device doesn\'t support the Depth API.\nSwitch to Canvas mode in Settings.
This device does not have ARCore installed or is not supported. Install ARCore from the Play Store to use AR features.
Camera permission is required for AR mode.
Camera isn\'t delivering frames — leaving AR. Reopen AR to retry with a safer camera mode.
Tap directly on your painted reference marks on the screen. The app will immediately isolate them.
Is the artwork on the correct wall?

The anchor is placed from where you stood when you captured. ARCore picks the tracked vertical surface most directly in front of that original position.

1. Return to approximately where you stood during capture
2. Slowly pan the camera across your mural wall so ARCore can register it as a flat surface
3. Hold steady facing the artwork, then tap below

The orange border will jump to the newly detected surface.
Tap a surface to place anchor.

Project your artwork onto a real wall or a canvas using AR or a basic camera overlay. 

Quickly create a mockup on a photo to show how the completed work will look. 

Or trace an image onto paper by using your device as a lightbox.

Tap New above to start your first mural.

You'll be taken into the editor where you can:
 
 • Add artwork from the Design menu
 
 • Switch to AR mode to project onto a real wall
 
 • Use Overlay to float your design on the live camera
 
 • Use Mockup to preview on a wall photo

 • Use Lightbox to trace your design onto paper
Move device slowly to recover tracking
Slowly rotate 360° to map your surroundings (%1$d°)
Too dark. Turn on the flashlight.
Walk left and right to build map
Mode Switcher: Switch between app modes. Instructions: Tap to expand, then select AR, Overlay, Mockup, or Lightbox.
AR Mode: Project design onto a wall using spatial mapping. Instructions: Scan environment until wall is detected, then tap to place the anchor.
Overlay Mode: Display design over live camera without spatial tracking. Instructions: Manually scale and move the design to fit your frame.
Mockup Mode: Preview design on a static photo. Instructions: Use \'Wall\' to set a background image, then arrange layers on top.
Lightbox Mode: High-brightness tracing surface. Instructions: Place paper over the screen and trace. Triple-tap anywhere to exit.
Target Management: Tools for AR anchors. Instructions: Tap to access tools for creating or re-aligning the mural\'s position.
Scan Resolution: Switch between Mural (large scale) and Canvas (small scale). Mural uses Voxel-based tracking for walls; Canvas uses Point Clouds for detailed desk-scale art.
Anchor Offset Handoff: High-precision point cloud tracking for large-scale murals. Instructions: Complete 360-scan, then tap to place anchor.
Create Anchor: Photograph a mark on the wall to lock projection. Instructions: Point at a distinct texture and tap. The app will track this mark.
Layer Management: Add images, text, or sketches. Instructions: Tap to expand. Long-press any layer button to open its secondary menu.
Import Image: Add a sketch or reference photo. Instructions: Select an image from your gallery. It will appear as a new design layer.
New Sketch: Add a blank layer for freehand drawing. Instructions: Select the layer to reveal brush, eraser, and smudge tools.
Add Text: Create a typography layer. Instructions: Tap the layer\'s \'Edit\' button to change content and formatting.
Change Wall: Set background for Mockup mode. Instructions: Select a photo of the physical wall you plan to paint on.
Project Management: Save, load, and export work. Instructions: Tap to access administrative tasks for your mural.
New Project: Start a fresh mural. Instructions: Tap to reset. Warning: This clears all current work. Save first!
Save Project: Store mural and AR map. Instructions: Enter a project name and tap Save to preserve your progress.
Project Library: Access saved murals. Instructions: Browse the library to resume work or import shared project files.
Export Image: Save high-res snapshot. Instructions: Renders all visible layers into a single PNG in your photo gallery.
App Settings: Configure preferences. Instructions: Open to adjust handedness, units, and scanning parameters.
Flashlight: Toggle device LED. Instructions: Use this to illuminate dark walls for better AR tracking.
Lock/Freeze: Prevent accidental changes. Instructions: In Trace mode, disables screen. In other modes, locks layer transforms.
Layer \'%1$s\': Active mural element. Instructions: Tap to select. Drag button up/down to reorder. Transform on screen with gestures.
Edit Text: Change words in typography layer. Instructions: Tap to open the keyboard and update the text content.
Brush/Text Size: Adjust scale and softness. Instructions: Drag up/down for size. Drag left/right for brush feathering.
Font Picker: Select typography style. Instructions: Tap to browse available fonts and apply them to the text layer.
Color Tool: Set active color. Instructions: Drag up/down for brightness, left/right for saturation. Tap for full color wheel.
Text Kerning: Adjust letter spacing. Instructions: Drag left/right to tighten or loosen the text layout.
Bold Toggle: Thick font weight. Instructions: Tap to toggle bold styling on the active text layer.
Italic Toggle: Slanted styling. Instructions: Tap to toggle italic styling on the active text layer.
Text Outline: Character stroke. Instructions: Tap to toggle a visible outline around the text characters.
Drop Shadow: Text depth. Instructions: Tap to toggle a soft shadow behind the typography for better legibility.
Generate Stencil: Create printable templates. Instructions: Tap to begin multi-layer stencil generation for this image.
Blend Mode: Composite style. Instructions: Tap to cycle through modes like Multiply, Screen, and Overlay.
Image Adjustments: Fine-tune appearance. Instructions: Tap to reveal sliders for Opacity, Brightness, Contrast, and Saturation.
Invert Colors: High-contrast negative. Instructions: Tap to flip colors. Whites become black and vice-versa—useful for tracing.
Color Balance: RGB channel tuning. Instructions: Tap to reveal sliders for precise Red, Green, and Blue adjustment.
Eraser Brush: Remove layer content. Instructions: Paint on screen to erase. Use \'Size\' button to adjust diameter.
Blur/Smudge Tool: Blend colors. Instructions: Paint over regions to soften edges or smudge details together.
Warp Tool: Reshape design. Instructions: Drag on screen to push and pull pixels—great for adjusting proportions.
Dodge Tool: Lighten areas. Instructions: Paint over parts of the layer to increase their luminance.
Burn Tool: Darken areas. Instructions: Paint over parts of the layer to decrease their luminance.
Isolate Subject: Auto-background removal. Instructions: Tap to extract the main subject from its background using AI.
Sketch Outline: Line art filter. Instructions: Tap to convert the photo into a black-and-white transparent outline.

string-array.name: onboarding_trace
string-array.item: This is Trace mode.
Arrange and edit your photos, sketches, and text by pressing the Design button.
When they are where and how you want them, press the Freeze button to turn your phone into a lightbox for tracing onto paper.
We recommend using either Trace mode or Mockup mode for editing.
That way your imagery is all ready to go and your phone can focus on augmenting reality when you press the AR button.

string-array.name: onboarding_ar
string-array.item: AR Projection — projects your design onto a real wall using the phone\'s depth sensor and spatial tracking.
Point at the wall and move slowly side-to-side. Coloured shapes appear as the engine maps the surface. Wait until the wall area is covered.
Just tap the screen. The app photographs the wall, extracts tracking features, and locks the anchor instantly.
Press \'Design\' to access your layer tools. Use \'Image\' to import photos, \'Draw\' to sketch, or \'Text\' to type.
Tip — blank white walls, smooth concrete, and single-colour surfaces won\'t track. Aim your anchor point at graffiti, rough plaster, or any distinct mark.

string-array.name: onboarding_overlay
string-array.item: Live Overlay — your design floats over the live camera view, no surface scanning needed.
Press \'Design\' to access layer tools. Use \'Image\' to import photos, \'Draw\' to sketch, or \'Text\' to type.
Pinch to scale, drag to reposition. The overlay stays fixed to your screen — step back to see how it fits the wall.

string-array.name: onboarding_ar_unavailable
string-array.item: This device doesn\'t support ARCore, so the AR Projection mode isn\'t available.
You can still use Trace, Mockup, and Live Overlay — every mode except AR works without ARCore.
Tap to dismiss.

string-array.name: onboarding_mockup
string-array.item: Photo Mockup — preview your design on a photo before you ever pick up a brush.
Open \'Design\' and tap \'Wall\' to choose or take a photo of your surface. Do this first!
Use \'Image\', \'Draw\', or \'Text\' to add layers on top of the wall. Pinch and drag to size and position them.

string-array.name: onboarding_design
string-array.item: This is Design — your workspace. Whatever you build here carries over into every other mode.
Open \'Design\' and add a layer: \'Image\' to import a photo, \'Sketch\' to draw, or \'Text\' to type.
Tap a layer to open its tools — grouped into Paint, Retouch, Adjust and Effects folders. Pinch and drag on the canvas to size and place it.
When it looks right, choose how to use it: \'Trace\' to turn your phone into a lightbox, \'Mockup\' to preview on a photo, or \'AR\' to project it onto a wall.

string-array.name: onboarding_design_layers
string-array.item: Open — import a photo or artwork; it lands as a new layer.
Sketch — start a blank layer and draw your own marks.
Text — type words and style them as their own layer.

resources.string: Locks your projection to a specific spot on the wall. Once set, the design stays in place even if you walk around.
The wall needs visible texture — graffiti, rough plaster, brick, or stickers all work well. Smooth, blank, or single-colour walls won\'t hold an anchor.
After tapping, you\'ll briefly see green blobs. Those are the tracking keypoints the app extracted. More blobs = stronger lock.
Add a sketch, photo, or reference from your gallery. It appears as a new layer stacked on top of the others.
Pinch with two fingers to resize. Drag with one finger to reposition. The layer snaps to the projected wall surface in AR mode.
In Lightbox (Trace) mode: disables touch so you can lay paper on the screen without accidentally moving anything.
In AR, Overlay, and Mockup modes: locks layer transforms so designs can\'t be accidentally moved or scaled.
Converts your image into printable spray-paint stencils. Each stencil represents one colour layer of the design.
A 3-colour design produces 3 stencil sheets. More colours = more sheets. Simple high-contrast images give the best results.
Each stencil exports as a PDF. Print at 100%% scale (no scaling) to get accurate life-size dimensions.
Opacity controls how transparent the layer is. Brightness shifts the overall lightness — useful to match the layer to your wall\'s tone.
Contrast separates darks from lights — great for making stencil outlines pop. Saturation controls colour intensity; drag to zero for greyscale.
Uses AI to automatically remove the background, leaving only the main subject of the image.
Works best on photos with a clear subject against a plain or contrasting background. Complex scenes with busy backgrounds may need manual touch-ups with the eraser.
